mission.title: knative-eventing: Expression language for Trigger filter
mission.description: Expression language for Trigger filter. Requested by 8+ users.

steps.title: Check current knative-eventing deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative-eventing version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
helm list -n knative-eventing 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative-eventing installation.

steps.title: Review knative-eventing configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative-eventing configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get configmap -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative-eventing
```
**Problem**
Now the `Trigger` filtering abilities are poor, because they require direct match on specific `CloudEvent` attributes.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Expression language for Trigger filter
steps.description:
I agree with  and others on timing: This is important but seems like it can be a backward compatible addition post-v1. I welcome contrary opinions. :smiley:

I commented on the (in)feasibility of Javascript as a filtering language at
```yaml
apiVersion: eventing.knative.dev/v1beta1
kind: Trigger
metadata:
  name: my-service-trigger
spec:
  broker: default
  filter:
    predicate: my-extension-value == dev.knative.foo.bar || type == dev.knative.foo.bar
  subscriber:
    ref:
      apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1
      kind: Service
      name: my-service
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative-eventing -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative-eventing
kubectl get events -n knative-eventing --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Expression language for Trigger filter" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: I agree with  and others on timing: This is important but seems like it can be a backward compatible addition post-v1. I welcome contrary opinions.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: eventing.knative.dev/v1beta1
kind: Trigger
metadata:
  name: my-service-trigger
spec:
  broker: default
  filter:
    predicate: my-extension-value == dev.knative.foo.bar || type == dev.knative.foo.bar
  subscriber:
    ref:
      apiVersion: serving.knative.dev/v1
      kind: Service
      name: my-service

predicate:
  allOf:
   - properties:
       attribute-a:
         const: aaa
   - properties:
       attribute-b:
         const: bbb

# spitballing, this doesn't actually exist
spec:
  filters:
    attributes:
      type: BuiltIn
    javascript:
      type: Remote
      ref:
        apiVersion: v1
        kind: Service
        name: javascript-event-filter

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative-eventing installed or the issue environment reproducible.
